parentQuestion: A patient cannot detect vibration from a tuning fork placed on their ankle but can still detect it on their knee. Light touch is intact at both locations. Which receptor type is selectively impaired?

scaffolds.q: Different mechanoreceptors detect different types of touch.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Light touch is detected by Meissner's corpuscles and Merkel discs. Vibration is detected by Pacinian corpuscles. Different receptors, different jobs.

scaffolds.q: Pacinian corpuscles detect vibration (like from a tuning fork).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Pacinian corpuscles are deep, encapsulated receptors that respond to high-frequency vibration (40-400 Hz).

scaffolds.q: Meissner's corpuscles and Merkel discs detect light touch.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! These superficial receptors detect light touch and texture. They're sensitive to gentle pressure.

scaffolds.q: Vibration and light touch can be affected separately because they use different receptors.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Disease can damage one type of receptor or pathway while sparing others. This is called dissociated sensory loss.

scaffolds.q: In early neuropathy, vibration sense often fails before light touch.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Pacinian corpuscles (vibration) may be more vulnerable than light touch receptors. Vibration loss is an early sign of nerve damage.

scaffolds.q: Testing vibration at different body locations can map the progression of nerve damage.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Lost at ankle but present at knee? The damage reaches up to ankle level. A tuning fork becomes a diagnostic mapping tool.

scaffolds.q: The patient's preserved light touch but lost vibration indicates selective damage to vibration-sensing receptors.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Different receptors, different fates. Pacinian corpuscles are damaged while light touch receptors survive.

scaffolds.q: Selective loss of vibration with preserved light touch indicates Pacinian corpuscle impairment.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Pacinian corpuscles detect vibration. When they fail, vibration sense is lost while light touch (different receptors) remains.

scaffolds.q: The receptor type selectively impaired is the ___.
scaffolds.explain: Pacinian corpuscles! These detect vibration. Lost vibration + preserved light touch = selective Pacinian damage.
